Calculated from Total Expenditure 15,139,992 and Depreciation 129,889 on page 7: (15,139,992 - 129,889) / 12.

Principal activities: The Association is a non-profit organisation. Its principal activities are: (a) to raise awareness of animal welfare issues; (b) to reduce the large number of dogs and cats that are abused, abandoned or destroyed everyday; (c) to provide proper treatment and medication to animals; and (d) to plan, organise and implement re-homing and/or adoption programmes for animals.

PUC Academy - a government-endorsed homeless animal welfare centre set to open in June 2026. PUC Academy will serve as a safe haven for up to 100 homeless dogs and 150 homeless cats, providing a state-of-the-art environment for rehabilitation and care.

significant_events.summary: Pre-construction works for a new government-endorsed animal welfare centre, PUC Academy, designed to house up to 100 dogs and 150 cats.

significant_events.event_name: Ongoing Animal Rescue, Rehabilitation, and Rehoming Programs

source.quote: Principal activities: The Association is a non-profit organisation. Its principal activities are: (a) to raise awareness of animal welfare issues; (b) to reduce the large number of dogs and cats that are abused, abandoned or destroyed everyday; (c) to provide proper treatment and medication to animals; and (d) to plan, organise and implement re-homing and/or adoption programmes for animals.

significant_events.summary: Continuous efforts to rescue abandoned/abused dogs and cats, provide medical treatment, foster care, and facilitate adoptions in Hong Kong.

data.original_text: Paws United Charity, founded in 2018, is dedicated to creating a brighter future for animals in need.
Our mission is to rescue, rehabilitate, and rehome abandoned and abused pets, giving them a second chance at life.
Through community outreach, education, and partnerships, we strive to raise awareness about animal welfare and promote responsible pet ownership.

PUC Academy - a government-endorsed homeless animal welfare centre set to open in June 2026. PUC Academy will serve as a safe haven for up to 100 homeless dogs and 150 homeless cats, providing a state-of-the-art environment for rehabilitation and care.
With your support, we can provide professional training for suitable rescue dogs to visit hospitals, elderly homes, and community centres as part of our animal therapy and outreach initiative.
Research shows that animal-assisted therapy can reduce stress, lower blood pressure, and ease feelings of loneliness among patients and the elderly.
By becoming a monthly donor with just $100, you can help feed hungry strays living in their colonies and protect them from illness.

With a monthly donation of $500, you can support the education efforts at our new PUC Academy. Your generosity helps us organise school tours and hands-on learning experiences that teach children the importance of responsible pet ownership and animal care—both at home and in the wild.
We will be aunching a new training program at PUC Academy to give rescued dogs a second chance at life—by transforming them into therapy dogs who bring comfort and joy to those in need.
Our core mission is to improve the lives of animals in our community by promoting responsible pet ownership, humane treatment of animals and choosing adoption instead of purchase.

Rescue: We rescue animals from various channels, including Government kennels, families who can no longer keep their pets, breeders who no longer want the animals, abandoned animals on the streets and born strays.

Rehabilitate: We provide medical treatment for all rescue animals, including costly surgical procedures, vaccinations, as well as de-flea and de-sex procedures. We make sure the animals are placed in foster homes that offer a safe and welcoming environment to rehabilitate them mentally and physically.

Re-home: Our end-goal is to successfully find forever homes for all of the animals that we rescue. Our volunteers carefully screen and guide interested parties through the adoption process so that both adopting families and animals can go home happy.
Paws United Charity is a non-profit, all-volunteer rescue organization established and registered in Hong Kong.
Target Beneficiaries. Nature and Environment. Sustainable Development Goals. SDG15: Life on Land.
